behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is key-selection logic in one command write path. MOCK-MISS: this behavior is about choosing artifact key, not cross-module state propagation. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP: no dependence on model output quality. Rejected higher level (Integration): behavior text is a single-stage write target check ("stores grounding into ... `grounding`, not `plan`") with no required multi-module handoff to reproduce failure.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke evaluate-stage write function with a fake in-memory artifact sink; assert exactly one write to `grounding` and zero writes to `plan`.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: This is module handoff: implement flow + artifact store read/write semantics. MOCK-MISS: unit-testing a single helper can miss wiring mistakes between reader and writer components. REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced with in-process store; no System need. LLM-DEP rejection evidence: text focuses on "reads grounding" and "writes ... to `plan`" (orchestration), not evaluating model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run implement-stage orchestration with an in-memory artifact repository preloaded with `grounding`; verify implement reads that artifact and emits output artifact only to `plan`.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: Bug appears at side-effect boundary: deep-dive decision logic plus artifact writer interaction. Unit-only logic checks can miss unintended write calls in orchestration path. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP rejection evidence: behavior is about suppressing redundant artifact write when diagnosis exists, not about correctness of model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Execute deep-dive stage with issue content containing diagnosis and an artifact store containing `plan`; assert deep-dive does not write/overwrite `plan`.

behaviors.description: Artifact lineage is preserved so implement can read the `grounding` artifact produced earlier in the same run context.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is cross-stage state handoff within run-context storage. This is beyond one local function and needs wiring of lineage metadata + retrieval. REAL-INFRA: in-process context/store is sufficient; no System requirement. LLM-DEP rejection evidence: text concerns artifact availability lineage, not model-output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Within one run context, simulate prior grounding write from evaluate stage, then invoke implement stage and assert it resolves that exact grounding artifact via lineage/context identifiers.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires full multi-step agent sequence and verifies end-result carries earlier grounding across stages. MULTI-STEP: yes, multiple agentic steps in sequence. LLM-DEP: "final implementation plan reflects grounding" depends on model decisions in final generation, not just plumbing. Rejected lower level (Agentic): text explicitly scopes to full chain (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), so single-step agentic test is insufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real chained agent workflow end-to-end with seeded issue context that yields identifiable grounding; assert final implement plan includes/uses that grounding signal from earlier stage in the same run.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: This is fallback orchestration plus artifact-store integrity across reads/writes; needs multiple modules (implement flow + repository state). REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP rejection evidence: behavior validates safe fallback and artifact integrity, not semantic quality of model output. Rejected Workflow: no required multi-step chain is specified here; it is single-stage fallback behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run implement stage with run context lacking `grounding` and preexisting unrelated artifacts; assert a valid `plan` artifact is produced and unrelated artifacts remain unchanged.
